description: Producer did:web resolution MUST reject an HTTP redirect to the SAME host but a DIFFERENT port. Per the same-authority definition (RFC-ACDP-0006 §7.5, cross-referenced from RFC-ACDP-0008 §4.8), two URLs share an authority only when scheme, host, and effective port all match. A redirect of a DID-document fetch from https://agents.example.com/.well-known/did.json to https://agents.example.com:8443/.well-known/did.json changes the effective port and therefore the service boundary; a host-only comparison would wrongly follow it.
ssrf_rule: RFC-ACDP-0008 §4.8 (Producer DID resolution SSRF protection — same-authority = scheme + host + effective port, RFC-ACDP-0006 §7.5)

input.scenario: Resolver fetches a producer DID document; the server returns a 30x whose Location header keeps the host but changes the port

additional_test_cases.note: explicit :443 is the SAME effective port as the https default — same authority, redirect MAY be followed

expected.behavior: The DID resolver MUST refuse the cross-port redirect. The authority comparison MUST be over scheme + host (case-insensitive) + effective port. A host-only comparison is non-conformant. The refusal is permanent and producer-caused — key_resolution_failed (HTTP 400), NOT key_resolution_unreachable (HTTP 502).
expected.non_conformant_implementation: A redirect check that compares only the hostname and treats a :8443 redirect target as same-authority with the :443 origin.
expected.rationale: A different port is a different service boundary; conflating the two re-opens the SSRF path the redirect cap is meant to close.
